title: My Years Are Mine. They Are Good.
subtitle: An affirmation for claiming your age without apology
excerpt: A short affirmation for claiming your age: not minimising it, not performing discomfort about it, not performing pride about it. For the woman who is tired of the cultural performance around getting older.

content.text: A short affirmation for claiming your years without performance. The culture around women and ageing runs in two directions: the deprecating joke and the forced pride. Both are performances. This affirmation is a quiet claim instead: the years are yours, they are part of you, they are worth having.
Say it standing, both feet on the floor. Three times, at speaking pace:
My years are mine. They are good. I am allowed to be this age without apology.

Three clauses. Ownership: the years are yours, not the culture's to evaluate. Quality: they are good, a factual statement about experience accumulated. Permission: no apology is required for having arrived here.
